artwork_description: In *1879*, Alfred Sisley (1839-1899) realized *A Courtyard in Chaville* in *Oil on canvas*. Attention to balance and interval—between figures and ground, or between mass and void—creates the work’s quiet authority. The composition’s internal logic invites viewers to read the image as an arrangement of relations, not simply a scene. *A Courtyard in Chaville* stands as a focused statement of the artist’s formal intelligence.
